facts.report_excerpt: = Vendoring Report :description: Current owner-oriented maturity report for the Vendoring component. :page-slug: vendoring-report This page is the current owner-oriented maturity report for *Vendoring*. It is written not as a defect log, but as a public-facing readiness portrait: what the component already demonstrates, what maturity band it currently occupies, and which leader-class direction it is converging toward. == Component Portrait Vendoring is not shaped as a simple supplier directory or a lightweight vendor CRUD layer. It reads as a Symfony-oriented vendor operations component: a system intended to manage vendor transaction runtime slices, statements, payouts, persistence contracts, and vendor-facing operational flows with release-candidate quality discipline. The component already shows signs of being treated as a governed subsystem rather than as a convenience feature. Its current shape suggests an internal vendor operations and transaction-governance platform layer designed for durability, structured evolution, and integration into a wider ecosystem. == Ecosystem Role Within the broader Smartresponsor direction, Vendoring appears to occupy the role of a vendor operations and supplier-runtime component. Its ecosystem role can be understood across several functions: * as a vendor transaction surface, * as a statement and payout operations layer, * as a persistence and runtime-governance surface, * as a release-candidate evidence and quality discipline surface, * and as a reusable vendor-facing capability for broader transactional systems. This makes Vendoring strategically more important than a typical supplier registry. It points toward a reusable internal vendor capability that other components can depend on. == Current Maturity Vendoring already presents several strong signals of maturity: * a clear vendor-operations product identity, * explicit Symfony 8 and PHP 8.4 targeting, * unusually deep release-candidate quality and canon scripting, * multiple test bands, * vendor transaction, statement, and payout surfaces, * and a headless/backend-first posture with release-facing evidence artifacts. In its current public reading, the component is no longer in a prototype-only band. It already behaves like a structured component moving toward release-candidate maturity. == RC Scorecard [cols="2,1,4",options="header"] |=== | Layer | Score to RC | Reading | Product identity | 8.5 / 10 | The component is already clearly framed around vendor-facing operational flows rather than a generic supplier data helper. | Architecture and domain shape | 8.6 / 10 | Vendor transactions, statements, payouts, persistence contracts, and backend runtime posture already form a coherent operating surface. | Runtime and application surface | 8.4 / 10 | Symfony runtime, forms, routing, mailer, CSRF, OpenAPI, and operator-facing minimal surfaces already establish a real vendor application layer. | QA maturity | 9.2 / 10 | The quality contour is exceptionally strong, including release-candidate lanes, contract tests, smoke surfaces, persistence tests, docs evidence, and canon reports. | Governance and operational readiness | 9.1 / 10 | Canon checks, config drift reports, mirror enforcement, RC evidence generation, and runtime smoke surfaces form one of the strongest governance postures among the components. | Vendor transaction and payout posture | 8.5 / 10 | Vendor transaction runtime slices, statement flows, payout surfaces, and transaction persistence signals are already concrete and substantial. | Market-facing alignment | 8.2 / 10 | The component already aligns well with enterprise vendor-management and supplier-operations classes, though it still reads more as a governed platform layer than a finished public procurement shell. | Overall readiness to RC | 8.8 / 10 | Vendoring is already a very strong pre-RC component with unusually mature internal quality and operational signals. |=== == Leader Line Vendoring should not be measured against basic supplier lists or simple payment helper modules. Its natural comparison line is closer to the enterprise-class supplier management, vendor operations, and supplier-runtime category, including the kind of capability bands represented by: * supplier information and vendor operations management, * statement and payout workflows, * transaction discipline and persistence integrity, * supplier runtime oversight and quality governance, * and governed operational handling of vendor-facing processes. This means the component is better understood as targeting the same strategic class of problem addressed by stronger supplier-management and vendor-operations leaders, while expressing that class through a Symfony-native internal component. In that sense, the leader line is not another supplier directory. It is closer to: * enterprise vendor operations class, * enterprise supplier-management class, * enterprise transaction-and-payout governance class, * reinterpreted as an internal ecosystem …

key_file_excerpts.composer.json:
{
    "name": "vendoring/vendor",
    "description": "Vendoring component",
    "type": "project",
    "license": "proprietary",
    "require": {
        "php": "^8.4",
        "ext-json": "*",
        "doctrine/dbal": "^4.2",
        "doctrine/doctrine-bundle": "^3.2",
        "doctrine/orm": "^3.3",
        "doctrine/persistence": "^4.0",
        "symfony/console": "^8.0",
        "symfony/event-dispatcher-contracts": "^3.6",
        "symfony/framework-bundle": "^8.0",
        "symfony/http-foundation": "^8.0",
        "symfony/mailer": "^8.0",
        "symfony/mime": "^8.0",
        "symfony/routing": "^8.0",
        "symfony/uid": "^8.0",
        "symfony/var-exporter": "^8.0",
        "twig/twig": "^3.15",
        "symfony/twig-bundle": "^8.0",
        "symfony/form": "^8.0",
        "symfony/validator": "^8.0",
        "symfony/security-csrf": "^8.0",
        "nelmio/api-doc-bundle": "^5.3",
        "ext-pdo": "*"
    },
    "require-dev": {
        "doctrine/doctrine-fixtures-bundle": "^4.2",
        "fakerphp/faker": "^1.24",
        "friendsofphp/php-cs-fixer": "^3.66",
        "phpstan/phpstan": "^1.12",
        "phpunit/phpunit": "^11.5",
        "symfony/panther": "^2.2"
    },
    "autoload": {
        "psr-4": {
            "App\\": "src/"
        }
    },
    "autoload-dev": {
        "psr-4": {
            "App\\Tests\\": "tests/",
            "Doctrine\\Bundle\\FixturesBundle\\": "stubs/Doctrine/Bundle/FixturesBundle/",
            "Faker\\": "stubs/Faker/",
            "Symfony\\Component\\Panther\\": "stubs/Symfony/Component/Panther/"
        }
    },
    "config": {
        "sort-packages": true,
        "allow-plugins": {
            "php-http/discovery": true
        }
    },
    "scripts": {
        "cs:check": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.php --dry-run --diff",
        "cs:check-quiet": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.php --dry-run",
        "cs:fix": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.php",
        "docs:rc-evidence": "@php bin/generate-rc-evidence.php",
        "lint": "@php tools/php/php84.php tools/qa/VendoringPhpLint.php",
        "lint:php": "@php tools/php/php84.php tools/qa/VendoringPhpLint.php",
        "md": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpmd src text phpmd.xml.dist",
        "md:tests": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpmd tests text phpmd.xml.dist",
        "phpstan": "@php tools/php/php84.php vendor/bin/phpstan analyse -c phpstan.neon --memory-limit=1G",
        "pipeline:local": "powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ci/run-vendor-local-pipeline.ps1",
        "pipeline:local:full": "powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ci/run-vendor-local-pipeline.ps1 -IncludeSmokes -IncludeReports",
        "pipeline:local:strict": "powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/ci/run-vendor-local-pipeline.ps1 -FailOnErrors",
        "qa:canon": [
            "@report:canonical-structure",
            "@report:mirror-enforcer",
            "@report:config-guard",
            "@report:config-drift",
            "@report:php-surface"
        ],
        "qa:full": [
            "@qa:style",
            "@qa:static",
            "@qa:smell",
            "@qa:test",
            "@qa:canon"
        ],
        "qa:smell": [
            "@md",
            "@md:tests"
        ],
        "qa:static": [
            "@stan"
        ],
        "qa:style": [
            "@lint",
            "@cs:check"
        ],
        "qa:test": [
            "@test"
        ],
        "quality": [
            "@test:symfony-stack",
            "@test:di",
            "@test:entrypoint",
            "@lint:php",
            "@test:compat",
            "@test:smoke",
            "@phpstan",
            "@test:mail",
            "@test:statement-command",
            "@test:statement",
            "@test:payout",
            "@test:repository",
            "@test:fixtu

verdict.summary: Vendoring is a Symfony 8/PHP 8.4 vendor-operations component now tagged v1.0.0-RC1 with strong QA/governance and deployment scaffolding indicating solid RC readiness.
strengths: Tagged v1.0.0-RC1 with structured release scaffolding
Symfony 8 + Doctrine ORM baseline and coherent config/routing/services
Deep QA toolchain (phpstan, phpunit, Panther, Playwright) and scripted quality lanes
Robust governance/ops scaffolding (.gate, .deploy, compose) and RC evidence generation
risks: CI execution and enforcement details not visible in this scan
Observability and SLO/health posture not evidenced
Public-market packaging/story remains internal-platform oriented (proprietary license)
next_actions: Ensure GitHub Actions run qa:full and Playwright tests on PRs/main
Publish RC evidence in CI artifacts and refresh Antora report to reflect RC1
Document health/readiness endpoints and baseline SLOs
Add logging/metrics/tracing wiring examples to deploy charts and compose
Clarify product positioning and integration boundaries in README/Antora
Export and publish OpenAPI spec artifact from CI
notes: Assessment grounded in current repo tree and existing Antora report; deeper signals will be captured by the scheduled runner.
Scores largely preserved from prior snapshot; overall adjusted to reflect the RC1 tag.

product.summary: Product identity is clearly vendor-operations–oriented and now backed by an RC1 tag and curated report/docs.
risks: Positioning still reads as internal platform rather than a public product
improvements: Tighten product pitch and feature matrix in README/Antora and link RC evidence

architecture.summary: Symfony 8 + Doctrine 3 with explicit bundles/routing/services and vendor-focused configs indicate a coherent domain shape.
risks: Bounded contexts and domain boundaries are not explicitly documented
improvements: Document aggregates/bounded contexts and persistence contracts in Antora

runtime.summary: Symfony runtime (console, routing, forms, mailer, CSRF) with Docker Compose and smoke surfaces establishes a real application surface.
risks: Health/SLO posture and capacity guidance not evidenced in this scan
improvements: Add health/readiness endpoints documentation and integrate k6 smoke into CI artifacts

qa.summary: Strong QA lanes with phpstan/php-cs-fixer/PHPUnit/Panther/Playwright, phpunit.xml.dist, gitleaks, and scripted qa:* targets.
risks: CI enforcement of all lanes (including Playwright) not confirmed in this scan
improvements: Ensure GitHub Actions execute qa:full and Playwright tests and publish coverage/reports

ops.summary: Governance and operational scaffolding are unusually strong with .gate policy/quality, .deploy (docker/helm/systemd), Compose, and RC evidence generation.
risks: Observability (metrics/logging/tracing) wiring not surfaced; secrets/ops runbooks not visible
improvements: Add observability configuration examples and document secrets/environment policy and runbooks

market.summary: Alignment targets enterprise vendor-operations as an internal platform; license is proprietary and public packaging is not emphasized.
risks: External go-to-market and partner-facing packaging remain unclear
improvements: Clarify ecosystem fit and integration paths in Antora/README and consider partner-facing docs

overall.summary: Tagged v1.0.0-RC1 with strong QA/governance and deployment scaffolding, the component demonstrates solid RC readiness.
risks: RC discipline depends on CI enforcement and documentation staying current
improvements: Connect scheduled assessment runner, enforce CI gates, and publish RC evidence artifacts in the report
